rulings.Case Title: KAZIM Z ACAR VS. JENNIFER E CRANE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant And Cross-Complainant Jennifer E. Cranes Motion To Reclassify Action From Limited To Unlimited Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 05, 2017, line 1. DEFENDANT JENNIFER CRANE's Motion To Reclassify Action From Limited To Unlimited Jurisdiction DENIED without prejudice. Moving party failed to address and sustain its burden under CCP 430.040(b)(2), i.e. failed to show good cause for not seeking reclassification earlier. = (501/NJL)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Noah J. Lebowitz

rulings.Case Title: ADA SORTO ET AL VS. WEDGEWOOD, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 05, 2017, line 3. DEFENDANTs SHELDON T. FONG AS TRUSTEE OF SHELDON T. FONG, UNITED THREE GROUPS, INC.'s MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC. DENIED. The Court has previously denied good faith settlement determination without prejudice because settling party failed to adequately address Tech-Built factors, specifically, "a rough approximation of plaintiff's total recovery." In this motion, moving party failed to do this again. Moving party's calculation of Plaintiff's total recovery in this action is limited to rent reimbursement, lost wages and property damage during the time of moving defendants' ownership. This does not account for all items included in "plaintiff's total recovery" in this action, e.g. recovery as to other defendants, attorneys' fees, emotional distress, etc. = (501/NJL)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Noah J. Lebowitz

rulings.Case Title: VINCENT T.C. TAI VS. JOHN M. SANGER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 05, 2017, line 4. DEFENDANTs JOHN SANGER and CATHERINE SANGER's Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Counsel GRANTED. No opposition filed. Plaintiff's counsel failed to meet his obligations under CCP 128.7(b)(3) and maintained this action after being informed that allegations of the discovery of easement recordation have no evidentiary support. Plaintiff's counsel shall compensate Defendant for reasonable attorney's fees incurred in filing answer and motion for summary judgment. = (501/NJL)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Noah J. Lebowitz

rulings.Case Title: VINCENT T.C. TAI VS. JOHN M. SANGER ET AL
